EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong tropical wave located few hundred miles east of the lesser Antilles became better organized on July 8, 2013 and was declared a Tropical Depression. Later, The National Hurricane Center named it as Tropical Storm Chantal. Chantal move generally west-northwest across the Caribbean Sea, passing arounf 150 miles south of Puerto Rico July 9, 2013. The outer bands associated with the circulation of Chantal, produced numerous showers with thunderstorms with strong gusty winds. Observed precipitation across Puerto Rico, Vieques, Culebra and the U. S. Virgin Islands showed that most rainfall was below an inch however totals were in excess ofthree inches in parts of Vega Baja, Vega Alta, Dorado and Toa Alta. No flooding was reported across the islands and official river gages did not approach flood stage at any time.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Power lines downed at Road PR-765, Barrio Atravezada Km 1.1 in the municipality of Caguas were reported due to strong gusty winds.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A very strong Tropical Wave moved over Puerto Rico during July 18, 2013. This wave brought deep topical moisture from the Intertropical Convergence Zone(ITCZ) through the area. These features, in combination with a southeast wind flow and local effects produced torrential rainfall, mainly along the north coast of Puerto Rico. Streamers from El Yunque National Forest developed in the afternoon on July 18, 2013, and were moving very slowly toward the northwest, which affected the San Juan Metropolitan area. The streamers redeveloped over the same area, and brought with it torrential rainfall which led to serious flooding across the San Juan Metro Area. The total rainfall accumulation exceed 9 inches over San Juan. Rainfall totals between 3 to 5 inches were also reported across the North Central section of Puerto Rico. Several reports of flooding were received from the municipalities of San Juan, Carolina, Bayamon and Guaynabo with hundreds of residences and roads affected by the waters. Overall losses were estimated over 1 million dollars across the San Juan Metropolitan area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Emergency management personnel reported Rio Cibuco out of its banks and flooded roads 620 and 647.

The streamers redeveloped over the same area, and brought with it torrential rainfall which led to serious flooding across the San Juan Metro Area. The total rainfall accumulation exceed 9 inches over San Juan. Rainfall totals between 3 to 5 inches were also reported across the North Central section of Puerto Rico. Several reports of flooding were received from the municipalities of San Juan, Carolina, Bayamon and Guaynabo with hundreds of residences and roads affected by the waters. EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong tropical wave located few hundred miles east of the lesser Antilles became better organized on July 8, 2013 and was declared a Tropical Depression. Later, The National Hurricane Center named it as Tropical Storm Chantal. Chantal move generally west-northwest across the Caribbean Sea, passing arounf 150 miles south of Puerto Rico July 9, 2013. The outer bands associated with the circulation of Chantal, produced numerous showers with thunderstorms with strong gusty winds. Observed precipitation across Puerto Rico, Vieques, Culebra and the U. S. Virgin Islands showed that most rainfall was below an inch however totals were in excess ofthree inches in parts of Vega Baja, Vega Alta, Dorado and Toa Alta. No flooding was reported across the islands and official river gages did not approach flood stage at any time.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Two lanes at road PR-948 were closed due to a landslide at Barrio Quebrada Grande in the municipality of Las Piedras.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Fuel dryness and enhanced sea breezes along with strong east southeast trade winds favored the development of fires mainly along the southern slopes of Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Grass fires were reported between the municipalities of Guayama and Salinas.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A deep surface low pressure across the Central Atlantic generated large long period north swells of 9 to 12 feet at 13 to 16 seconds across the local Atlantic waters and Caribbean Passages. These long period swells resulted in very large breaking waves between 13 to 16 feet locally higher along exposed reefs with dangerous rip currents.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Barrio Bajuras at road 485 in Camuy was reported flooded.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Large north northeast swells affected the Atlantic coastline, producing dangerous surf conditions along north facing coastlines of Puerto Rico and the northern U.S. Virgin Islands.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A person was dragged by a rip current. He was rescued later.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: North swells invaded the north coasts of Puerto Rico. This swells produced beach erosion affecting mainly the northeast facing coast of Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A bridge collapsed at road 951 in the municipality of Loiza due to High Surf conditions.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Low relative humidity values, combined with subsidence inversion and strong day time heating aid in the development of Wildfires across the southern slopes of Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Smoke plume associated with a Wildfire affected the Christian bilingual school at Parabueyon sector. 22 children were evacuated.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A frontal boundary affected the local area with scattered to numerous showers mainly along the eastern third of Puerto Rico. The heaviest rainfall amounts were observed over the municipalities of Fajardo, Ceiba, Luquillo, Rio Grande and Naguabo.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Road PR-191 was closed and impassable due to Rio Palmer out of its banks.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A frontal boundary affected the local area with scattered to numerous showers mainly along the eastern third of Puerto Rico. The heaviest rainfall amounts were observed over the municipalities of Fajardo, Ceiba, Luquillo, Rio Grande and Naguabo.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Ten people were rescued at Charco Frio in the border between Fajardo and Ceiba municipalities due to sharp river rises.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A frontal boundary affected the local area with scattered to numerous showers mainly along the eastern third of Puerto Rico. The heaviest rainfall amounts were observed over the municipalities of Fajardo, Ceiba, Luquillo, Rio Grande and Naguabo.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A young male was rescued at Rio Espiritu Santo when he was dragged by a water surge.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A frontal boundary affected the local area with scattered to numerous showers mainly along the eastern third of Puerto Rico. The heaviest rainfall amounts were observed over the municipalities of Fajardo, Ceiba, Luquillo, Rio Grande and Naguabo.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Exit 22 of Highway PR-53 toward road PR-31 was closed due to Rio Blanco out of its banks.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Lowest relative humidities were observed due to down slope winds over the northern coast. Minimum relative humidities ranged from 40 to 55 percent. Several smoke plumes were observed on radar.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A wildfire was reported at Camp Santiago in the municipality of Salinas.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Lowest relative humidities were observed due to down slope winds over the northern coast. Minimum relative humidities ranged from 40 to 55 percent. Several smoke plumes were observed on radar.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A forest fire was reported in Utuado.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A surge of low level moisture in the prevailing east northeast wind flow resulted in isolated to scattered showers with thunderstorms over the Western Interior section of Puerto Rico.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A very strong Tropical Wave moved over Puerto Rico during July 18, 2013. This wave brought deep topical moisture from the Intertropical Convergence Zone(ITCZ) through the area. These features, in combination with a southeast wind flow and local effects produced torrential rainfall, mainly along the north coast of Puerto Rico. Streamers from El Yunque National Forest developed in the afternoon on July 18, 2013, and were moving very slowly toward the northwest, which affected the San Juan Metropolitan area. The streamers redeveloped over the same area, and brought with it torrential rainfall which led to serious flooding across the San Juan Metro Area. The total rainfall accumulation exceed 9 inches over San Juan. Rainfall totals between 3 to 5 inches were also reported across the North Central section of Puerto Rico. Several reports of flooding were received from the municipalities of San Juan, Carolina, Bayamon and Guaynabo with hundreds of residences and roads affected by the waters. Overall losses were estimated over 1 million dollars across the San Juan Metropolitan area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: General Public reported heavy rain with 1.00 -1.24 inches in 15 minutes near the Los Colobos, Carolina.
